reasoning: The Lean statement matches the informal one exactly. `MetricSpace X` encodes "metric space X"; `IsCompact K`, `IsClosed F`, and `Disjoint K F` directly correspond to "K compact", "F closed", and "K and F disjoint" (for sets, Mathlib's `Disjoint` on `Set X` is `K ∩ F = ∅`). The conclusion `∃ δ : ℝ, δ > 0 ∧ ∀ p q, p ∈ K → q ∈ F → dist p q > δ` matches "there exists δ > 0 such that d(p, q) > δ if p ∈ K, q ∈ F", including the strict inequality as in Rudin. The encoding `∀ p q, p ∈ K → q ∈ F → ...` is the standard Lean rendering of "∀ p ∈ K, ∀ q ∈ F". No hypotheses are added or omitted and no types are changed.
